FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Bank earnings set to boom on SpaceX fees and volatility, but Fed rate-hike odds rising on geopolitical tension could pressure NIMs.

bullets.text: Q2 bank earnings poised to beat expectations, driven by SpaceX IPO advisory fees, geopolitical volatility trading, and commercial lending pickup.
Fed rate-hike odds for July rising after Strait of Hormuz oil-price spike—direct headwind to bank NIM compression and deposit-pricing dynamics.
Fed Governor Waller signals inflation broadening beyond energy/tariffs, keeping rate-hike door open despite recent disinflation momentum.
CFPB nominee Brian Johnson wins 60-day discretionary review period on staff cuts post-confirmation, giving Trump pick operational control over bureau workforce.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments goes non-profit under CalPERS; RIA consolidation heats up with leadership shifts and platform reorganizations; Betterment faces cash sweep litigation.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments for $580B pension system; Russell sheds debt albatross and converts to non-profit governance model, mirroring Vanguard's playbook.
RIA consolidation accelerates: Hightower's organic growth soars, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, Choreo closes 4th acquisition post-SpaceX, Avantis adds $50B+ AUM.
Aspen-backed Summitry ($4B RIA) founder Colin Higgins steps to chair; two executives elevated to co-CEOs after 22-year tenure.
Betterment sued in class action for allegedly sweeping client cash into non-interest-bearing accounts at partner banks, generating hidden firm profits.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Brazil's insurance market opens up as new law takes effect; Mile Auto expands via Georgia acquisition; Kenvue faces major litigation revival; insurers deploy AI and new capacity structures.

bullets.text: Brazil's new Insurance Law goes live with regulatory tailwinds—Uplift CEO signals market maturation, improved claims handling, and reinsurer appetite picking up.
Mile Auto acquires Georgia's Insurance House (~$100M premiums, 55k+ policyholders) for $100M-ish, hitting 10 states and 1,600 distribution partners by July 2026.
Federal appeals court revived 500+ Tylenol-autism lawsuits against Kenvue on July 13, overturning dismissal and exposing significant litigation risk.
Marsh launches MLOne, a US lead umbrella casualty facility to streamline excess claims coordination, complementing its existing BX1 (Bermuda) and MX1 (US) platforms.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: 5 stories spanning payments M&A, credit divergence, and regulatory headwinds across specialty finance.

bullets.text: Mastercard exploring Vocalink sale—potentially returning the 2016-acquired UK payments platform back to original banking owners.
CMBS special servicing rates climbed in June 2026 as retail, office, and lodging loans deteriorated—signaling workout activity ahead for servicers.
North America private debt remains LP-favored despite market noise, though AlbaCore flags widening gap between performing and distressed credits.
Klarna faces Dutch class action lawsuit and regulatory risk in key European market; Nubank gets Mexico banking license to offset geographic pressure.

PE & Deals.lede: PE financing and secondaries heat up: Arctos debuts novel $6.2B GP financing structure while Moelis and majors race to capture LP-led secondaries growth.

bullets.text: Arctos closes $6.2B GP financing using 'self-liquidating' Keystone structure—first deployment of this approach in GP-financing market, signaling demand for alternative capital solutions.
Moelis poaches Evercore team to build LP-led secondaries practice as market booms; LGT, Dawson, and Pomona actively sourcing deals amid favorable pricing and liquidity.
Sector rotation: Nordic Capital targets European water treatment; Korean pension (KBIZ) committing ~$230M to domestic PE/VC blind pools in 2026.

Markets & Macro.lede: Banking fees surging post-AI boom, but margin debt warnings and earnings uncertainty cloud the outlook.

bullets.text: US investment banking fees tracking toward highest annual total since 2022, driven by M&A revival and AI-fueled capital markets activity—a windfall for advisory shops.
Margin debt climbing as investors lever up to chase returns; Wall Street reading it as a late-cycle greed signal that historically precedes corrections.
Bank earnings kick off Tuesday with market braced for high expectations and potential disappointment—sets tone for financial sector outlook.
EU imported record LNG volumes from Russia before scheduled import ban, signaling energy-supply vulnerability and inflation risks for European FI clients.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings tomorrow set to reveal credit and deposit health; meanwhile, ETF flows turn negative and Chinese AI threatens US tech dominance.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—critical read on credit quality, deposits, and consumer health across lending, trading, and wealth management.
ETF flows went negative in a rare week; redemptions hit equity, bond, and alternative products as sentiment shifted.
Alibaba's AI model ranks first globally with cheaper Chinese alternatives undercutting US tech dominance in artificial intelligence.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 5 stories spanning AI economics, market theory, housing value, and policy blindspots—mostly intellectual frameworks rather than FIG-specific news.

bullets.text: Open-source AI now matches frontier models at 1/10th the cost, forcing a shift from model performance to durable competitive moats—relevant for fintech and banking tech stacks.
Tyler Cowen argues competitive markets depend on P ≠ NP: if collusion detection becomes computationally tractable, cartels become sustainable—a theoretical lens on market structure.

Noah Smith: suburbs deliver real economic value (affordability, schools, safety) despite urban elite dismissal—contextualizes consumer and demographic trends affecting real estate and consumer finance.

all.lede: Big week ahead: major bank earnings begin tomorrow while M&A and capital markets hit 4-year highs, with Mastercard exploring Vocalink exit and CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments.

bullets.text: Big Six US banks report Q2 earnings Tuesday–credit quality, deposit dynamics, and consumer health will be under the microscope as market braces for high expectations.
US investment banking fees tracking toward highest annual total since 2022, driven by M&A revival, IPO recovery, and debt capital markets on AI and consumer strength tailwinds.
CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments for portfolio expansion while Russell sheds debt and shifts to non-profit governance model, mirroring Vanguard's playbook.
Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink, the UK payments platform acquired from British banks in 2016, potentially returning it to original owners.
